formulas.name: Boyle's Law
formulas.variables: P1 and P2: pressure at initial and final stages, V1 and V2: volume at initial and final stages
formulas.units: Pascal (Pa) for pressure, cubic meter (m^3) for volume
formulas.notes: States that, at constant temperature, the volume of a given mass of an ideal gas is inversely proportional to its pressure.

formulas.name: Charles' Law
formulas.variables: V1 and V2: volume at initial and final stages, T1 and T2: temperature in Kelvin at initial and final stages
formulas.units: cubic meter (m^3) for volume, Kelvin (K) for temperature
formulas.notes: States that, at constant pressure, the volume of a given mass of an ideal gas is directly proportional to its temperature in Kelvin.

formulas.name: Avogadro's Law
formulas.variables: V1 and V2: volume at initial and final stages, n1 and n2: number of moles at initial and final stages
formulas.units: cubic meter (m^3) for volume, mole (mol) for number of moles
formulas.notes: States that, at constant temperature and pressure, equal volumes of gases contain equal numbers of molecules.

formulas.name: Gay Lussac's Law
formulas.variables: P1 and P2: pressure at initial and final stages, T1 and T2: temperature in Kelvin at initial and final stages
formulas.units: Pascal (Pa) for pressure, Kelvin (K) for temperature
formulas.notes: States that, at constant volume, the pressure of a given mass of an ideal gas is directly proportional to its temperature in Kelvin.

formulas.name: Bernoulli's Principle
formulas.variables: P: pressure, ρ: density, v: velocity, g: acceleration due to gravity, y: height
formulas.units: Pascal (Pa) for pressure, kilogram per cubic meter (kg/m^3) for density, meter per second (m/s) for velocity, meter per second squared (m/s^2) for acceleration due to gravity, meter (m) for height
formulas.notes: States that, for an ideal fluid, the sum of the pressure and the kinetic energy per unit volume plus the potential energy per unit volume remains constant.

formulas.name: Poiseuille's Law
formulas.variables: Q: flow rate, r: radius of the tube, ΔP: pressure difference, η: dynamic viscosity, L: length of the tube
formulas.units: cubic meter per second (m^3/s) for flow rate, meter (m) for radius, Pascal (Pa) for pressure difference, Pascal-second (Pa*s) for dynamic viscosity, meter (m) for length
formulas.notes: States that, for laminar flow in a cylindrical tube, the flow rate is directly proportional to the fourth power of the tube's radius and the pressure difference, and inversely proportional to the dynamic viscosity and the tube's length.

formulas.name: Mean Circulatory Pressure
formulas.variables: MCP: mean circulatory pressure, P_a: arterial blood pressure, P_v: venous blood pressure
formulas.units: millimeter of mercury (mmHg) for blood pressure
formulas.notes: States that, the mean circulatory pressure is the average pressure throughout the circulatory system.

formulas.name: Total Peripheral Resistance
formulas.variables: R_T: total peripheral resistance, P_a: arterial blood pressure, P_v: venous blood pressure, Q: cardiac output
formulas.units: millimeter of mercury (mmHg) for blood pressure, liter per minute (L/min) for cardiac output
formulas.notes: States that, the total peripheral resistance is the opposition to the flow of blood in the systemic circulation.
